title: Rich Savory Canadian Lentil Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew made with fermented lentils, malai, and a blend of spices, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This shojin-friendly dish is steam-cooked to perfection.
instructions: Rinse the fermented lentils and drain well.
In a large pot, saute the onion, garlic, carrots, and celery in a little water until tender.
Add the malai, vegetable broth, thyme, and bay leaves to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and steam for 30 minutes.
Add the lentils to the pot and continue to steam for another 20 minutes.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
Refrigerate or freeze any leftovers for up to 5 days.
Reheat the stew gently before serving.
